normalized.rawData:
================================================================
PUNJAB REVENUE AUTHORITY
LAHORE PROPERTY MARKET ANALYSIS REPORT
Q4 2025 / Q1 2026 OUTLOOK
================================================================

REPORT ID: PRA-LHR-2026-Q1-001
DATE OF PUBLICATION: January 5, 2026
CLASSIFICATION: Public Release
CREDIBILITY: Government Authority (High)

================================================================
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
================================================================

The Lahore property market demonstrated robust performance in 2025,
with average property prices increasing by approximately 18% year-
over-year across all major housing societies and commercial zones.

This growth was driven by strong demand from overseas Pakistanis,
infrastructure development including the Orange Line Metro extension,
and favorable demographic trends in the 25-40 age bracket.

================================================================
KEY FINDINGS
================================================================

1. PRICE PERFORMANCE
   - Average price per marla in DHA Phase 6: PKR 18,000,000
   - Year-over-year growth: 18.2% (based on registered transactions)
   - Bahria Town Lahore growth: 16.7%
   - Gulberg and Model Town: 10-12% growth
   - Johar Town: 11.4% growth

2. TRANSACTION VOLUME
   - Total registered property transactions in Lahore: 45,230
   - Increase over 2024: 8.5%
   - Average transaction value: PKR 25,000,000

3. RENTAL MARKET
   - Average rental yield across Lahore: 2.5%
   - DHA Phase 6 rental yield: 1.9-2.1%
   - Bahria Town rental yield: 2.3-2.5%
   - Johar Town rental yield: 3.0-3.5% (highest yield area)

4. SUPPLY AND DEMAND
   - New housing units delivered in 2025: 12,500
   - Estimated housing demand: 18,000 units per year
   - Supply deficit: approximately 5,500 units
   - Vacancy rates remain low at 7-8% in premium areas

================================================================
MARKET OUTLOOK 2026
================================================================

We project continued strong growth in Lahore property prices:
- Expected price appreciation: 15-18% in 2026
- DHA and Bahria Town to lead growth
- Commercial property expected to outperform residential
- Rental yields expected to stabilize at current levels

KEY GROWTH DRIVERS:
- Continued infrastructure investment (Ring Road, Metro extensions)
- Overseas Pakistani remittances (projected $35B nationally)
- Population growth and urbanization (Lahore growing at 3.5% annually)
- Government housing incentives under Naya Pakistan Housing scheme
- Expected SBP rate cuts making mortgages more affordable

================================================================
RISK FACTORS
================================================================

- Political instability could deter overseas investment
- Inflation remains elevated at 12%
- Documentation requirements may slow cash transactions
- Global economic slowdown could reduce remittances
- Potential oversupply in apartment segment in DHA Phase 8-9

================================================================
INVESTMENT RECOMMENDATION
================================================================

Based on our analysis, Lahore property remains an attractive
investment opportunity with expected returns of 15-18% (capital
appreciation) plus 2-3% (rental yield) for a total return
potential of 17-21% annually.

We rate Lahore property as BUY for investors with:
- Budget: PKR 20,000,000+
- Time horizon: 3-5 years
- Risk tolerance: Moderate to High
- Liquidity needs: Low (property is illiquid)

================================================================
METHODOLOGY NOTE
================================================================

This report is based on:
- 12,450 registered property transactions in Q3-Q4 2025
- Price data from Punjab Land Revenue Authority (PLRA)
- Survey of 200 licensed property dealers
- Macroeconomic data from State Bank of Pakistan

Note: Prices used are registered prices which may differ from
actual transaction prices. Registered prices tend to be higher
than market transaction prices due to documentation requirements.

================================================================
DISCLAIMER
================================================================

This report is for informational purposes only. The Punjab Revenue
Authority does not provide investment advice. Property investment
involves risks including potential loss of capital. Past performance
does not guarantee future results.

Report prepared by: Economic Analysis Division
Punjab Revenue Authority, Lahore
Contact: analysis@pra.punjab.gov.pk
================================================================

normalized.sourceName: Lahore Property Market Shows Signs of Cooling - Pakistan Real Estate Weekly

claims.context: Article claims Lahore property growth has slowed to ~8% YoY, citing independent analysis
DHA Phase 6 grew only 8.2% YoY in Q4 2025, down from 19.2% in Q4 2024
Transaction volumes declined by 12% compared to same period last year
Rental yields compressed to 2.0% across premium locations
New housing supply increased by 25% as developers complete delayed projects
Bahria Town Lahore shows 10.5% growth per article analysis
Borrowing costs remain elevated at 17.5%
Article predicts 6-8% annual growth, below inflation (negative real return)
New apartment complexes in DHA and Bahria increased inventory by 30%

normalized.rawData:
Lahore Property Market Shows Signs of Cooling After Years of Rapid Growth

By Aisha Malik | January 10, 2026 | Pakistan Real Estate Weekly

Market Analysis

After several years of double-digit growth, Lahore's property market is showing clear signs of cooling. Our analysis of recent transaction data suggests that year-over-year price growth has slowed to approximately 8%, a significant decline from the 15-20% gains seen in 2022-2024.

Key Findings

  • Average property prices in DHA Phase 6 grew only 8.2% YoY in Q4 2025, down from 19.2% in Q4 2024
  • Transaction volumes have declined by 12% compared to the same period last year
  • Rental yields continue to compress, now averaging just 2.0% across premium locations
  • New housing supply has increased by 25% as developers complete delayed projects
  • Bahria Town Lahore shows slightly better performance at 10.5% growth but still below historical averages

Market Drivers

Several factors are contributing to the slowdown:

  • Interest rate environment: Despite SBP cuts, borrowing costs remain elevated at 17.5%
  • Oversupply: New apartment complexes in DHA and Bahria have increased available inventory by 30%
  • Regulatory changes: New tax documentation requirements have discouraged cash-based transactions
  • Alternative investments: Stock market rally and high bond yields are diverting capital from property

Price Predictions

We expect Lahore property prices to grow at 6-8% annually over the next 2-3 years, which is below inflation and represents a negative real return for many investors. Investors seeking higher returns should consider diversifying into REITs, mutual funds, or government bonds, which currently offer more attractive risk-adjusted returns.

Contradicting Reports

Note: The Punjab Revenue Authority's recent report claimed property prices grew by 18% in 2025. However, our independent analysis based on actual transaction data from 500+ verified sales shows growth closer to 8%. The discrepancy may be due to the PRA's reliance on listed prices rather than actual transaction prices, and their sample being skewed toward high-end properties in newer developments.

Disclaimer: This analysis is based on data from approximately 500 verified transactions and may not represent all market segments. The author is not a licensed financial advisor. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

Published: January 10, 2026

Source credibility: Independent blog, unaudited data
